articles.title: EUR/CAD Rally Continues as ECB Officials Prepare Markets for June Hike
articles.expand: EUR/CAD extended its near term rally today as increasingly hawkish rhetoric from senior European Central Bank officials continued pushing markets toward pricing a June rate hike, while falling oil prices added renewed pressure on Canadian Dollar. The pair resumed its rebound from 1.5941 and is now approaching the key 1.6148 resistance zone, with br...

articles.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Dropped the Hammer With This 11-Word Statement at His Swearing-in Ceremony -- and Wall Street Isn't Ready for It
articles.expand: Key PointsJerome Powell's term as Fed chair ended on May 15, with President Trump's handpicked successor, Kevin Warsh, officially taking the reins.

articles.title: Fed’s Kashkari Warns of Rising Inflation Risks, But Says It’s Too Soon to Predict Rate Hike
articles.expand: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari warned that inflation risks are becoming increasingly concerning as the Middle East conflict continues pushing energy and supply-chain costs higher across the global economy. Speaking in Tokyo, Kashkari said “most of the data has said the inflationary risks are higher, not lower.”. Kashkari argued that ...

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar sticks to hawkish RBNZ-led gains; Iran risks cap further upside
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair catches aggressive bids in reaction to the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's (RBNZ) hawkish on-hold rate decision on Wednesday, reversing the previous day's losses to a nearly one-week low.

articles.title: Speech: Economics and the Public Good
articles.expand: Speech by Carolyn Hewson AO, Monetary Policy Board member, to the Adelaide University Joseph Fisher Public Lecture

articles.title: US Dollar: Consumer clouds over hike pricing – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Molly Schwartz notes that United States (US) Treasury yields were mostly flat with a slight bull-steepening bias, while the US Dollar Index (DXY) was little changed. She highlights that the US OIS curve still prices a high probability of further rate hikes.

articles.title: Australian Dollar: Softer CPI backs RBA pause – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur contrasts the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) with the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ), noting Australia has already hiked three times and that April Consumer Price Index (CPI), including a monthly fall and lower annual rate, supports a pause.

articles.title: Fed's Kashkari says far too soon to predict what the next policy move should be
articles.expand: Fed should have neutral policy outlook going forward Risk to US inflation is now higher than risk of labour market deterioration That said, need to still pay attention to both risks Most US data released since April has shown inflationary risks are higher, not lower Inflationary shockwave sent across the globe from the Middle East war could persist...

articles.title: AUD/NZD Price Forecast: Retreats below 1.2200 on hawkish RBNZ, weak Australian data
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar (AUD) is dropping sharply against the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) on Wednesday.

articles.title: Fed’s Kashkari: Risk to US inflation now higher than risk of deterioration in labour market
articles.expand: Minneapolis Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank President Neel Kashkari said in the European trading session on Wednesday that the major concern for the central bank now is higher United States (US) inflation than deteriorating labor market conditions; however, the central bank needs to pay attention to both

articles.title: Swiss Franc: Intervention stance offsets safe-haven appeal – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank's Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley highlights that despite resilient Swiss growth and firm PMI readings, very low inflation leaves little need for imminent SNB tightening.

articles.title: AUD/JPY Price Forecast: Declines on softer Australia's CPI, but broader bullish trend remains intact
articles.expand: The AUD/JPY cross attracts some sellers to around 113.95 during the early European trading hours on Wednesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) softens against the Japanese Yen (JPY) as Australian Consumer Prices Index (CPI) inflation rose by less than expected in April.

articles.title: Gold struggles near $4,500 as bulls remain on the sidelines amid hawkish rate expectations
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) remains on the back foot heading into the European session on Wednesday and seems vulnerable near the $4,500 psychological mark as persistent geopolitical uncertainties continue to support the safe-haven US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Holds gains above 1.3800 as Oil prices weigh on Loonie
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) is trading practically flat against the Canadian Dollar (CAD) on Wednesday, consolidating gains above 1.3800 at the time of writing, after having rallied about 1.7% over the previous three weeks.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar: Hawkish RBNZ repricing path – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Volkmar Baur at Commerzbank notes the RBNZ held rates at 2.25% with a hawkish split vote, pushing market odds of a July hike above 70%.

articles.title: Euro drifts higher against British Pound on hawkish ECB signals
articles.expand: The EUR/GBP cross trades with mild gains near 0.8650 during the early European trading hours on Wednesday. Hawkish comments from the European Central Bank (ECB) policymakers provide some support to the Euro (EUR) against the British Pound (GBP).

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP edges higher reversing previous day’s corrective slide
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair attracts some dip-buyers during the Asian session on Wednesday and stalls the previous day's retracement slide from the vicinity of a nearly two-week top, levels just above the 1.3500 psychological mark.

articles.title: AUD/NZD Correction Risk Growing After RBNZ and Australia CPI, But Confirmation Still Missing
articles.expand: AUD/NZD may finally be running into a wall. After months of powerful upside momentum, today’s combination of a hawkish RBNZ shock and softer Australian inflation data delivered the strongest challenge yet to the pair’s medium-term uptrend. The correction risks are clearly growing — but markets still are not fully convinced the reversal has truly st...

articles.title: Nasdaq analysis today, even a week after IPO frenzy and before a "deal with Iran today"
articles.expand: Nasdaq futures hold bullish structure, but cooling, as AI IPO frenzy of last week added another Wall Street risk-on narrative. Today we have a new "deal with Iran" narrative. Nasdaq futures remain structurally bullish, but NQ is now pressing into an important resistance zone near 30118-30159. The broader story is not only technical. A Financial Tim...

articles.title: Euro advances on hawkish sentiment surrounding ECB outlook
articles.expand: EUR/USD gains ground after registering minor losses in the previous day, trading around 1.1640 during the Asian hours on Wednesday. The pair gains upward momentum, heavily supported by a wave of hawkish sentiment surrounding the European Central Bank’s (ECB) monetary policy outlook.

articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Hawkish RBNZ-led rally stalls near 0.5880, ahead of 200-SMA on H4
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair regains positive traction during the Asian session on Wednesday and rallies to the 0.5875-0.5880 resistance zone in reaction to the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's (RBNZ) hawkish on-hold rate decision.

articles.title: Kiwi Rallies As RBNZ Turns Up The Hawkish Heat
articles.expand: The RBNZ held its rates steady at 2.25%, but a committee split and hawkish guidance sent NZD surging. Here’s what new traders need to know.

articles.title: Swiss Franc edges higher as fading risk aversion weighs on USD
articles.expand: USD/CHF inches lower after registering modest gains in the previous day, trading around 0.7850 during the Asian hours on Wednesday.

articles.title: RBNZ Delivers Hawkish Hold as Split Vote Signals Rate Hikes Coming Soon
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand left the Official Cash Rate unchanged at 2.25% today, but the decision delivered a distinctly hawkish message as policymakers signaled that rate hikes are likely coming sooner and more aggressively than previously expected. In a major surprise, the Monetary Policy Committee split evenly 3-3, forcing Governor Anna Bre...

articles.title: Australia CPI Slows More Than Expected to 4.2% as Fuel Inflation Eases
articles.expand: Australia’s headline inflation unexpectedly softened in April, reinforcing the view that the Reserve Bank of Australia may remain cautious about delivering further rate hikes despite still-elevated core price pressures. Headline CPI slowed from 4.6% yoy to 4.2% yoy, below expectations of 4.4% yoy. On a monthly basis, CPI rose 0.4% mom, undershootin...

articles.title: Breman Speech: RBNZ Governor sheds light on policy outlook after the hawkish interest rate hold
articles.expand: Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s (RBNZ) Governor Anna Breman presents the prepared remarks on the Monetary Policy Review (MPR) and responds to media questions at the press conference after the May monetary policy announcement.

articles.title: Australia April CPI slows to 4.2% but core inflation creeps to highest since 2024
articles.expand: Australia's April CPI slowed to 4.2% annually, undershooting forecasts, as a fuel excise cut weighed on headline, but the trimmed mean core measure edged up to 3.4%, its highest since late 2024. Summary: Source: Australian Bureau of Statistics, April 2026 monthly CPI indicator Headline CPI rose 0.4% in April, slowing the annual pace to 4.2% from 4....

articles.title: NZD jumps - RBNZ holds OCR at 2.25% by casting vote as committee splits 3-3 on rate hike
articles.expand: The RBNZ held its OCR at 2.25% today by the governor's casting vote after a 3-3 committee split, signalling rates will need to rise sooner and by more than expected as inflation heads for 4.3%. Summary: Inflation, not the labour market, is the greater risk to the US economy and the Fed should adjust its stance soon to avoid falling behind the curve Citade...

articles.title: New Zealand Kiwi waits on an RBNZ boxed in by its own rate cuts
articles.expand: There is something almost comic about a central bank that spent a year insisting rates needed to come down, only to find itself staring at inflation heading the wrong way.

articles.title: Premium Watchlist Recap: U.K. CPI Report (April 2026)
articles.expand: April inflation in the United Kingdom missed expectations at 2.8%, but shifting geopolitical tensions and surprisingly hawkish central bank commentary helped Sterling avoid any significant directional breakdowns.
